headline: Nepal Uses AI to Protect Crops From Crop-Raiding Macaques
meta_description: Farmers in Nepal are testing AI technology to detect and deter macaques from raiding maize fields, reducing crop damage.
summary_short: Farmers in Nepal are gaining a powerful new ally in their fields: artificial intelligence. AI systems now help detect macaques before they raid crops, giving villagers b…
body:
In the villages of central Nepal, dawn has long meant a tense vigil. Farmers stand guard over maize fields, eyes scanning the treeline for movement, knowing that even a brief absence can mean considerable crop damage when macaques arrive. The burden of protection has fallen heavily on families who cannot afford to lose their harvest.

Now, AI technology is beginning to change that equation. Instead of relying solely on human vigilance, villages are testing systems that can detect macaques approaching the fields. The technology offers farmers a chance to respond before damage occurs, rather than simply managing loss after the fact.

This application of artificial intelligence represents a practical solution to a very real problem. For farming communities where crops are survival, the ability to protect yields through smarter technology can mean the difference between a successful season and hardship. It is a reminder that innovation need not be distant or abstract—sometimes it simply means bringing a new tool to an old challenge.
